quotes: Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

rules.intent: Maintain the established brand color distribution balance across all materials.
rules.rule_text: Apply the palette in the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio where Brand Blue (#00529b) and Gold (#ffc60a) dominate, while tertiary colors function purely as accents or background surfaces.

quotes: Headlines/headings: Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds)
Body text: Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds
Headlines/headings: Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
Body text: Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).

rules.intent: Ensure text legibility and contrast against dark or light background states.
rules.rule_text: Headlines must use Brand Blue (headline.color = #00529b) on light backgrounds, or White (#ffffff) on dark backgrounds. Body text must use Graphite (body.color = #212121) on light backgrounds, or White (#ffffff) on dark backgrounds.

quotes: Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures
Charts/about:blank Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.

rules.intent: Standardize chart styling to prevent visual confusion and emphasize crucial clinical metrics.
rules.rule_text: Charts must use Brand Blue (#00529b) and Sky Blue (#358CCB) as dominant colors, with Gray used for placebo representation. Gold/Sunshine (#ffc60a/#faa31b) is strictly reserved for highlighting and emphasizing key data figures.

quotes: Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts)
Callout surfaces: Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Differentiate quantitative data boxes from narrative layout blocks using background indicators.
rules.rule_text: Callout background fills must map to their content type: data callouts use Light Blue (#E6F0F9) at 70% tint (opacity.logo.gold = 0.7), and text-heavy callouts use Golden Sand (#FEF1C8) at 30% tint (opacity.callout.golden_sand_box = 0.3).

quotes: never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data
**Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

rules.intent: Prevent high-attention accent colors from diluting primary CTAs or creating clinical confusion.
rules.rule_text: Coral (#F26A38) must only be used as a sparing tertiary accent; it must never be used for primary CTAs, headlines, or data displays. Avoid using Coral in email sections unless explicitly instructed by the brief.

quotes: Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Maintain high visual accessibility standards and standard interactive patterns across light and dark frames.
rules.rule_text: Inline links must be underlined. Links use Brand Blue (body.link.color = #00529b) on light backgrounds, and White (#ffffff) on dark backgrounds.

quotes: 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances
The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

rules.intent: Ensure trademark assets are locked to exact regulatory/brand guidelines.
rules.rule_text: The brand logo mark's 70% transparency (opacity.logo.gold = 0.7) over Gold (#ffc60a) must be strictly maintained in all scenarios, including when the logo mark is integrated as an background pattern or decorative layout graphic.
